{"fixed_xml":"<ITEM>Bey Herrn Johannes Christ seel. Frau Wittib ist zu haben: <BIBL><AUTHOR>Johann Arnds</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Passions- und Oster-Predigten</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to</FORMAT> <PRIZE>fl. 2.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Predigten von denen Egyptischen Plagen</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to</FORMAT> <PRIZE>24. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Stahlii</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Fundamenta Chymiae dogmat. rat. & experim.</TITLE> <VOLUME>Pars II.</VOLUME>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> <PRIZE>fl. 1. 30. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Poetevin</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Nouvelle Grammaire Francoise & Allemande</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo</FORMAT> <PRIZE>32. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Maiers der Geistl.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Krancken Geistl. oder Seelen-Cur.</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo</FORMAT> <PRIZE>48. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Hauss-Gebett-Buch, mit grober Schrifft</TITLE>, <VOLUME>2. Theile</VOLUME>, <FORMAT>8vo</FORMAT> <PRIZE>40. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL></ITEM>","number_of_corrections":8,"explanation":"1. Removed stray OCR artifacts "√†" (encoded as \u221a\u2020) that appeared between FORMAT and PRIZE tags in five BIBL entries — these were not meaningful content and disrupted the structure.

2. Fixed the closing tag in the third BIBL entry: the price was opened with <PRICE> but closed with </PRIZE>. Corrected the opening tag to <PRIZE> so it is consistent with all other price tags in the file.

3. Corrected OCR corruptions in several words caused by the "√ü" (\u221a\u00fc) artifact, which represents a long-s/ß ligature:
   - "Pa√üions-" → "Passions-" (in the first TITLE)
   - "Hau√ü-Gebett-Buch" → "Hauss-Gebett-Buch" (in the last TITLE)

4. Corrected the author name "Stahiii" → "Stahlii" (Georg Ernst Stahl, author of Fundamenta Chymiae), and "Nouvelie" → "Nouvelle" in the Poetevin grammar title — both clear OCR misreadings.

All BIBL, AUTHOR, TITLE, FORMAT, VOLUME, and PRIZE tags were otherwise well-formed and properly nested, so no structural tagging additions were required beyond the price-tag fix."}

parsed.fixed_xml: <ITEM>Bey Herrn Johannes Christ seel. Frau Wittib ist zu haben: <BIBL><AUTHOR>Johann Arnds</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Passions- und Oster-Predigten</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to</FORMAT> <PRIZE>fl. 2.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Predigten von denen Egyptischen Plagen</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to</FORMAT> <PRIZE>24. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Stahlii</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Fundamenta Chymiae dogmat. rat. & experim.</TITLE> <VOLUME>Pars II.</VOLUME>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> <PRIZE>fl. 1. 30. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Poetevin</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Nouvelle Grammaire Francoise & Allemande</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo</FORMAT> <PRIZE>32. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Maiers der Geistl.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Krancken Geistl. oder Seelen-Cur.</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo</FORMAT> <PRIZE>48. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Hauss-Gebett-Buch, mit grober Schrifft</TITLE>, <VOLUME>2. Theile</VOLUME>, <FORMAT>8vo</FORMAT> <PRIZE>40. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL></ITEM>
parsed.explanation:
1. Removed stray OCR artifacts "√†" (encoded as \u221a\u2020) that appeared between FORMAT and PRIZE tags in five BIBL entries — these were not meaningful content and disrupted the structure.

2. Fixed the closing tag in the third BIBL entry: the price was opened with <PRICE> but closed with </PRIZE>. Corrected the opening tag to <PRIZE> so it is consistent with all other price tags in the file.

3. Corrected OCR corruptions in several words caused by the "√ü" (\u221a\u00fc) artifact, which represents a long-s/ß ligature:
   - "Pa√üions-" → "Passions-" (in the first TITLE)
   - "Hau√ü-Gebett-Buch" → "Hauss-Gebett-Buch" (in the last TITLE)

4. Corrected the author name "Stahiii" → "Stahlii" (Georg Ernst Stahl, author of Fundamenta Chymiae), and "Nouvelie" → "Nouvelle" in the Poetevin grammar title — both clear OCR misreadings.

All BIBL, AUTHOR, TITLE, FORMAT, VOLUME, and PRIZE tags were otherwise well-formed and properly nested, so no structural tagging additions were required beyond the price-tag fix.
